exercise.name: High Knees
exercise.whatItDoes: A high-energy cardio burst that strengthens the legs, improves heart rate, and enhances coordination and flexibility in the hip flexors.

executionSteps.step: Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Lift your right knee toward your chest as high as possible.
Immediately switch and lift your left knee while dropping the right.
Continue alternating knees at a running pace, pumping your arms to maintain momentum.

commonMistakes.mistake: Leaning too far back to compensate for leg height.
Landing heavily on your heels; stay light on the balls of your feet.

exercise.name: Jumping Jacks
exercise.whatItDoes: A classic full-body movement that improves cardiovascular endurance, coordination, and metabolic rate.

executionSteps.step: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump up while spreading your legs wider than shoulder-width and clapping your hands above your head.
Jump again to return your feet together and bring your arms back to your sides.
Maintain a steady, rhythmic pace throughout the set.

commonMistakes.mistake: Landing with locked knees; keep a slight bend to absorb impact.
Using "half-arms" instead of full range of motion over the head.

exercise.name: Lunges
exercise.whatItDoes: Develops lower body strength and stability by targeting the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes while improving balance.

executionSteps.step: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
Take a large step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are bent at approximately 90-degree angles.
Ensure your front knee is directly above your ankle and your back knee is hovering just off the ground.
Push off your front foot to return to the starting position and repeat with the other leg.

commonMistakes.mistake: Allowing the front knee to extend past the toes, which puts stress on the joint.
Losing balance by stepping in a straight line; keep your feet hip-width apart like they are on train tracks.

exercise.name: Abdominal Crunches
exercise.whatItDoes: Isolates the abdominal muscles to improve core strength and endurance.

executionSteps.step: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands lightly behind your head without pulling on your neck.
Engage your core to lift your shoulders toward your knees.
Lower slowly back to the start and repeat with control.

commonMistakes.mistake: Pulling on the neck instead of using the abs.
Lifting the entire back off the ground.

exercise.name: Plank
exercise.whatItDoes: Strengthens the entire core, including the abs, obliques, and lower back, while building stability.

executionSteps.step: Get into a forearm plank position with your elbows under your shoulders.
Form a straight line from your head to your heels.
Squeeze your glutes and pull your navel toward your spine.
Hold the position steady without letting your hips sag or hike up.

commonMistakes.mistake: Allowing the lower back to arch (swayback).
Dropping the head or shoulders.

exercise.name: Wall Sit
exercise.whatItDoes: Builds exceptional isometric endurance in the quadriceps and glutes.

executionSteps.step: Lean your back flat against a sturdy wall.
Walk your feet out and slide down until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Keep your knees aligned over your ankles at a 90-degree angle.
Maintain pressure through your heels while holding the position.

commonMistakes.mistake: Positioning feet too close to the wall, causing knee strain.
Not squatting deep enough to reach parallel.

exercise.name: Push-Ups
exercise.whatItDoes: Builds functional upper body strength in the chest, shoulders, and triceps while stabilizing the core.

executionSteps.step: Place hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor while keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Push through your palms to return to the starting plank position.
Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the move.

commonMistakes.mistake: Flaring elbows out to 90 degrees, which stresses the shoulders.
Letting the hips sag or the lower back arch.

exercise.name: Push-Up and Rotation
exercise.whatItDoes: Combines chest strength with rotational core stability and shoulder balance.

executionSteps.step: Perform a standard push-up.
As you push back up, shift your weight to one hand and rotate your body open.
Reach your top arm toward the ceiling, forming a "T" shape with your body.
Return to the starting position and repeat, alternating sides.

commonMistakes.mistake: Rotating only the shoulders instead of the entire torso and hips.
Moving too fast and losing control of the balance phase.

exercise.name: Side Plank
exercise.whatItDoes: Isolates the oblique muscles and improves lateral spinal stability and hip strength.

executionSteps.step: Lie on your side with your elbow directly under your shoulder and feet stacked.
Lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from head to feet.
Keep your core tight and your neck neutral, looking straight ahead.
Hold the position, then switch to the other side halfway through the set.

commonMistakes.mistake: Allowing the bottom hip to drop toward the floor.
Leaning the top shoulder forward rather than keeping it stacked vertically.

exercise.name: Squats
exercise.whatItDoes: Builds functional strength in the lower body, specifically the quads, hamstrings, and glutes, while improving hip mobility.

executionSteps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly pointed out.
Lower your hips back and down as if sitting into an invisible chair.
Keep your chest up and ensure your knees stay aligned with your toes.
Drive through your heels to return to a standing position.

commonMistakes.mistake: Allowing the knees to cave inward during the descent.
Lifting the heels off the ground, shifting weight too far forward.

exercise.name: Step-Ups
exercise.whatItDoes: Increases lower body power and balance while providing a significant cardiovascular boost.

executionSteps.step: Stand in front of a sturdy chair or step.
Place your entire right foot onto the chair and push through the heel to lift your body up.
Bring your left foot up to join the right on the chair.
Step back down carefully, starting with the right foot, and alternate legs.

commonMistakes.mistake: Pushing off the floor with the bottom foot instead of using the top leg's strength.
Using an unstable surface that could tip over.

exercise.name: Triceps Dips
exercise.whatItDoes: Targets the triceps on the back of the arms and the front of the shoulders for improved pushing strength.

executionSteps.step: Sit on the edge of a chair with hands next to your hips.
Slide your hips off the chair while supporting your weight with your arms.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach about 90 degrees.
Press back up until your arms are straight but not locked.

commonMistakes.mistake: Letting the elbows flare out to the sides; keep them tucked back.
Moving the hips too far away from the chair, which strains the shoulders.
